issue_title: Configuration does not fail for unrecognized parameters
issue_body: Passing an unrecognized parameter to a Configuration subclass does not explode, but should. In the presence of typed Configuration objects, ignoring unrecognized fields allows typos and other errors to slip by easily.

issue_title: Treat 3rdparty as a 1st class distinction amongst source roots.
issue_body: As noted [here](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/cd0ce6f5ae50f10ebdb7ca024a3e4bd4c840e08c/src/python/pants/source/source_root.py#L155-L155), the source root system only distinguishes between source and test roots today and this forces Go plugin users to manually define source roots for one.  Elevating  the distinction of a root that contains remote dependency information as first class should allow handling language integration like the Go contrib plugin that care about this distinction.

issue_title: Using sklearn2pmml with multiprocessing.pool blocks database connections
issue_body:
Hi,

I have set up a Python program that pulls data from the database using psycopg2 into pandas dataframes and then does random forest regression (via sklearn) on that data. To be able to use the fitted models elsewhere I wanted to use sklearn2pmml, but even just adding the import statement caused the code to stall on the database connection line.

For clarification, each thread has its own ` with psycopg2.connect(CONNECTION_STRING) as conn: ` statement, which is the point at which the program stalls.

issue_title: Make target customize_build leaves source tree dirty
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

Hmm, I just spent 2 hours debugging something that is really a harsh complexity -- I would argue that a default environment should not 1) call anything in `build_tools/customize_build.py` and 2) ship with something in `kolibri.utils.build_config.default_plugins`

We now maintain a list of plugins in:

1. The `~/.kolibri/kolibri_settings.json`
1. A `DEFAULT_PLUGINS` default variable in `kolibri.utils.conf`
1. Another `plugins` in `kolibri.utils.build_config.default_plugins`
1. Yet another default set of plugins in `build_tools/default_plugins.txt`

I couldn't build developer docs locally. This I thought had something to do with my path, and that Sphinx wasn't loading the right `kolibri`, but defaulting into some system wide path. Then I started investigating the `kolibri_settings.json` in `docs-developer/_build/kolibri_home` and found that it kept reverting to a state that didn't make any sense, until I found that it was an artifact from having previously built some stuff on an older branch.

One thing is that the `kolibri/utils/build_config` isn't cleaned by `make clean`, but another thing is that we are really building and shipping a Python module with configuration data in it and dynamically adding it to the sdist.

### Expected behavior

Me can run `make developer-docs` when I switch around between branches and do all the mandatory stuff with `pip install -r requirements/*` :)

### User-facing consequences

n/a

### Errors and logs

n/a

### Steps to reproduce

n/a

### Context

n/a

issue_title: Pull Dockers from private repos
issue_body:
Currently item_tasks can pull Docker containers from public DockerHub, but it would be desired by customers in the context of Algorithm-as-a-Service that they keep their code in private repos.

>For the moment, you could get around the problem by pulling the docker image manually on the server and setting pull=False when generating the task.

This issue can serve as a point of requirements gathering. This work should not be started until after 
https://github.com/girder/girder_worker/pull/201 lands, and should be based on `docker_run`.


# Potentially relevant background

https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/token/#requesting-a-token

If that helps, the related code is here:

  https://github.com/girder/girder_worker/blob/d14f7d8c7396de793b842e627bb45b95ec3219d6/girder_worker/plugins/docker/executor.py#L22-L32

and it is using "from_env" function to create the client.

  https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/client.html#docker.client.from_env

It looks like the the function may be useful:

  http://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/client.html#docker.client.DockerClient.login

...


```
We would need add support in girder_worker via the function that pulls the image.  I'm not sure where we would want the authentication information to come from.  I can imagine one of the following options:

1. Store the auth information with girder worker so that every pull uses that account.  This would then be a "site specific" docker account that would be used transparently.
2. Allow passing auth information via the girder endpoint in the worker plugin + requisite changes to the web client exposing an input form for this information.

The first option would be less work, but 2 would be more flexible.
```

For @sjh26's needs, option 1 above is ideal.


>(docker.client.DockerClient.login) supports passing a custom registry,
something that should also be exposed in the future. GitLab has its own container registry
(https://docs.gitlab.com/ce/user/project/container_registry.html) [which could be an option for a private container repo].



/cc @danlamanna @jbeezley @jcfr @kotfic @cdeepakroy @sjh26

issue_title: When should I invoke the close method for the connection object?
Making methods consistent in Bayesian and Markov models
issue_body:
Taking the example of adding Factor to Markov and CPD to Bayesian model, the `set_cpd` method can directly accept a 2D array whereas the `add_factor` method of Markov model accepts a `Factor` object. Making the arguments similar for both these methods would make it more intuitive to the user. 

I think we should change `set_cpd` to accept an object as argument because we also want the Bayesian Model to accept `TreeCPD` and `RuleCPD` objects as CPD.

Also in the case of Bayesian model we are asking for the name of states of the variables from the user whereas in case of Markov we are automatically assigning a numerical value to the states. 
So I think we should add the feature to accept state names to Markov models as well.

@abinashpanda @gargshashank5 :What do you think?

issue_title: Space are not allowed in glyph names: standard says they should be

the following quoted paragraph was copied from http://monotype.github.io/OpenType_Table_Source/otl_source.html#glyph-references

"Glyph References
Glyphs are usually represented by their name from the ‘post’ table or ‘CFF ‘ table. If there is no format 2 post table or no names stored in the ‘CFF ‘ table then it is possible to refer to glyphs by Unicode or by glyph index.

To refer to a glyph by Unicode use U or u followed by a space, followed by the hexadecimal Unicode. To refer to a glyph by index use a # followed by a space followed by the index in decimal. For example: in a normal Latin font in standard order the lowercase a can be referred to in the following ways:

a
U 0061
u 0061
# 68

issue_title: Download Fails Due to URL Parameters in File Names
issue_body:
🚨Please review the [Troubleshooting](../#troubleshooting) section
before reporting any issue. Don't forget to check also the current issues to
avoid duplicates.

### Subject of the issue
Downloading statistical-inference course fails midway with invalid argument error for file "C:\\My Courses\\statistical-inference\\01_week-1-probability-expected-values\\01_module-1-introduction\\03_syllabus_lectures.zip?raw=true", in downloaders.py

### Your environment
* Operating System (name/version): Windows 10
* Python version: 3.5.0
* coursera-dl version: github master HEAD (b8596ea30d567342fdde995851e51d09e05edead)

### Steps to reproduce
Run something like: coursera-dl -n "C:\Users\User\_netrc" --path "C:\Users\User\Documents\My Courses" statistical-inference

### Fix
Temporarily added a line in coursera_dl.py after line 523:

`lecfn = re.sub('\?.+', '', lecfn)`

This strips the URL parameter for now and the course downloads... but there are probably better places to do it!

issue_title: Current Pulp version on PyPI not installable
issue_body:
The current stable version of pulp on PyPI depends on an old version of pyparsing (for py2.x). A newer version of pyparsing is also used by setuptools, which is used to install the older version pyparsing when installed pulp. The old version of pyparsing doesn't work with new versions of setuptools, and fails to install the older pyparsing for pulp. 

Therefore pulp is not installable on python 2.7 with up-to-date setuptools.

I believe a new version of pulp needs to be released. This issue has been fixed in #107 so only a release needs to be created.

issue_title: Code smell in moving files to trash
issue_body:
I ran across this while debugging a conda build issue: https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda/install.py#L964-L965

wherein we basically create a tmp folder, like

c:\users\frank\miniconda\conda-bld\tmp4326jklasdf

Then append

..\long\other\path\to\conda-bld

to it.  So we toss the random dir we just added.  I have no idea why we don't just use the random dir.  I'll put in a PR if you agree this doesn't make sense.  Otherwise, I'd like to be enlightened.

issue_title: Invalid root_dir for pip installed conda on ubuntu 14.04
issue_body:
These lines https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4b51cd79bf17f59d1c1db95eeae539b4831baba9/conda/config.py#L115-L116 expect sys.prefix be the same as conda's prefix.  This is not true on ubuntu 14.04.

```
$ which python
/usr/bin/python
$ which conda
/usr/local/bin/conda
```

As a result, the symlink in an environment points to /usr/bin/conda, which does not exist.  The if-condition in https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4b51cd79bf17f59d1c1db95eeae539b4831baba9/conda/install.py#L387-L388 is True and the os.symlink call will raise an exception due to "OSError: File exists".

It seems like this can be workaround by setting CONDA_ROOT=/usr/local

issue_body:
This was already an issue that was closed: #242 https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/242

It happened to me again today, I forgot to put sudo in front of 'conda update conda' and something terrible happened. I'm not actually sure what specific line I messed up, but when I tried using conda or source activate I got the following error:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5668998/18779966/2c9a50ea-8130-11e6-8ddf-eb4062bd5033.png)

I then updated the shebang line, which as stated in Issue 242 had been corrupted to some nonsense.

Then I tried doing 'conda update openssl' without sudo and what do you know,
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5668998/18780009/5bdb08a4-8130-11e6-9222-33580da93d43.png)

everything is broken. So I'm working on fixing stuff now, but figured should let you guys know this is still potentially a problem.

issue_title: Regression in 4.3.27: mkl is installed even if nomkl is passed in the conda create arguments
issue_body:
Maybe there is another way to switch between MKL and not using MKL in the most recent conda version? I had a look in the [release notes](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md) and I could see things related to features in the upcoming 4.4 release but nothing in 4.3.27.


#### To reproduce:
This snippet downloads miniconda 4.3.21 and then shows the output of `conda create -n test numpy nomkl` with conda 4.3.25 and 4.3.27:

```bash
rm -rf /tmp/miniconda3 /tmp/Miniconda3*
```

issue_title: I can't activate environments in centrally administered install
issue_body:
I am getting this message

Cannot activate environment bash.
User does not have write access for conda symlinks.

when I activate an environment created in a shared account. 

Doing something like, from the administrator account,

conda create -n boostenv boost python=2.7

and then from a user account, doing

source activate boostenv

leads to that message. This is a very recent version of conda, 4.1.3 I think. This kind of thing was working with 4.0.3. Are we no longer able to make environments centrally for users, unless they can all write to the central location? That doesn't seem right - note, the filesystems may be different enough between the user home directory and the central location that a hard link is not supported, only soft links will work. I am on linux - rhel7.

issue_title: Using six instead of 2to3 for python 3 compatibility
issue_body:
With six, we can write code which is compatible between python2 and python3 without passing throw the a 2to3.
Using 2to3 can be a source of issues as if we do an import pypot without having called the setup.py, it won't works.
Whatever, it would be a step forward to having to write always python 3 compatible code.

I write this issue only to keep it in mind, but it is at a very low priority.

issue_title: name parser is not case-insensitive
issue_body:
If you have a `TEST-ME section` you can only jump to it via [CTRL]+[click] from `perform TEST-ME` but not from `perform test-Me`.

The same applies to variables.

Is this an pyqode (2.10.1) issue?

issue_title: Improper Node Addressing Returns None
issue_body: If a node is improperly requested from the node manager, None is returned. This should return an error to reduce confusion.

issue_title: shrink action's info output shows 'Node "{0}" ...' instead of actual node's name
issue_body:
shrink action's info output says:

```
2018-02-06 20:54:53,709 INFO      Node "{0}" has multiple data paths and will not be used for shrink operations.
2018-02-06 20:54:54,120 INFO      Node "{0}" has multiple data paths and will not be used for shrink operations.
2018-02-06 20:54:54,492 INFO      Node "{0}" has multiple data paths and will not be used for shrink operations.
2018-02-06 20:54:54,882 INFO      Node "{0}" has multiple data paths and will not be used for shrink operations.
```

This is because curator/actions.py, line 1918 is:

```
                self.loggit.info('Node "{0}" has multiple data paths and will not be used for shrink operations.')
```
It should be:

```
                self.loggit.info('Node "{0}" has multiple data paths and will not be used for shrink operations.'.format(name))
```

issue_title: Getting <type 'exceptions.KeyError'>: 'count' when using count filter
issue_body:
[Count Filter](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/curator/4.1/filtertype_count.html) doesn't seem to be working as expected in Curator **4.1**, given this configuration:

```
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_snapshots
    description: >-
      Delete all snapshots other than latest 5 (based on name) for backup
      snapshots. 
    options:
      repository: 'my_backups'
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: 'curator-'
    - filtertype: count
      count: 5
      reverse: True
```

Results in:

```
2016-09-23 10:42:50,007 ERROR     Failed to complete action: delete_snapshots.  <type 'exceptions.KeyError'>: 'count'
```

issue_title: Add information on how to use the example files
issue_body:
It seems that the example files are some yaml representation of cli commands.

However, I haven't found any documentation on how to use them. Maybe a simple README in the example directory, with the few lines explaining how to use them could be sufficient. I haven't found any tag/command in the cli tool allowing to take a yaml file instead of the command line.

I'm sure it is very simple, but I can't find it.

issue_title: "ipConfigurations" return null
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
ipConfiguration is returning null even if interface exist and public and private IP are set

**To Reproduce**
az network nsg list --query [*].[networkInterfaces]

**Expected behavior**
Would except to return the name, type ,private and public ip for each interfaces

issue_title: vm/vmss: expose data disk specific storage sku
issue_body:
So far, in the `vm/vmss create` commands, the data disk defaults to the same sku of the os disk, which is not always what users want. There are scenarios a user can have 10+ data disks, and like to configure individuals. So the proposed change after discussed with @singhkays are:
1. Expose a `--data-disk-storage-skus` which accepts space separated sku list
2. Argument values can be just sku name which CLI will match with the lun in sequence; or users can pair with lun, e.g. `2=Premium_LRS`. For disks w/o sku specified, we go with the one used by os disk for backward compatibility. 

Examples:
1. `az vm create -g rg -n vm1 --image centos --data-disk-sizes-gb 10 20 --data-disk-storage-skus standard_lrs  standard_lrs`
2. `az vm create -g rg -n vm2 --image imageWith16DataDisks  --data-disk-storage-skus 2=standard_lrs 10=standard_lrs`

For mixing styles, CLI will throw for simplicity, but can change if there are asks
`az vm create -g rg -n vm2 --image imageWith16DataDisks  --data-disk-storage-skus standard_lrs 10=standard_lrs`

issue_title: [Question] Should the VM create command create a new NSG when referencing an existing NIC?
issue_body:
If I call `az vm create`, and specify an existing NIC via the `--nics` flag, I notice that I still need to explicitly set the `--nsg ''` parameter if I want to prevent it from creating a new NSG.

This behavior seemed a little strange to me since [these docs](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-nsg) seem to indicate that the NSG is a NIC-associated resource. Therefore, if a user is explicitly managing the NIC, as opposed to relying on the `az vm create` uber command to create it (along with other resources), then it seemingly shouldn't create another NSG, under the assumption that they intend to manage the NSG as well. That may be the incorrect way to think about this, so I'm just thinking out loud here.

Calling `az vm create` with the `--nics` flag seems to suppress it from creating all other resources (e.g. public IP), so the NSG seems to be the only thing that requires being explicitly turned off. Ideally, I could call the following:

```shell
az vm create -g foo-group -n foo --image UbuntuLTS --nics foo-nic
```

As opposed to this:

```shell
az vm create -g foo-group -n foo --image UbuntuLTS --nics foo-nic --nsg ''
```

issue_title: Add __hash__ in Model
issue_body:
Currently defined in Ansible:
```python
# Quick 'n dirty hash impl suitable for monkeypatching onto SDK model objects (so we can use set comparisons)
def gethash(self):
    if not getattr(self, '_cachedhash', None):
        spec = inspect.getargspec(self.__init__)
        valuetuple = tuple([getattr(self, x, None) for x in spec.args if x != 'self'])
        self._cachedhash = hash(valuetuple)
    return self._cachedhash
```

This use the `__init__` signature, but we can use attribute map instead and don't use inspect. Or even use `as_dict`:
```python
def __hash__(self):
    if not getattr(self, '_cachedhash', None):
        self._cachedhash = hash(self.as_dict())
    return self._cachedhash
```

Need to check that all possible internal type are ok (duration, datetime, etc.) but should be fine

FYI @nitzmahone @johanste 

Related to https://github.com/ansible/ansible/pull/33624#issuecomment-352855396

issue_title: Error js
Spring2Constraint gets the dynamics wrong!
issue_body:
Hi! 

First of all, thanks for an awesome physics engine! I've been using Bullet for my research and noticed strange behavior of the btGeneric6Dof**Spring2**Constraint, which seemed to ignore an important principle of physics. I investigated this with a simple horizontal pendulum simulation: one dof (rotation about vertical axis, spring enabled, no gravity). I did several simulations with different pendulum length (distance from pivot point to mass). To my surprise, the frequency of oscillation did not change, no matter the length of the pendulum -> this is dynamically incorrect! The frequency should be inversely correlated with pendulum length. 
I simulated the same thing with the "depricated" btGeneric6Dof**Spring**Constraint and all of a sudden, without making any other changes, I could see the correct behavior: the frequency increased with decreasing pendulum length!

![constraints_frequency_length](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36479450/36342410-c2451dec-13c3-11e8-8657-b5d3ee1b7bb0.png)

If there are any parameters I don't know of that would make Spring2Constraint to behave correctly, I would love to know! Otherwise, it would be great if you could fix the issue in the Spring2Constraint since it effects the accuracy of the physics simulations significantly. To my knowledge, the issue only occurs when the spring is enabled - the frequency of a pendulum driven only by gravity changes with the length.

Thanks!

issue_title: Tags: an attempted overview
issue_body:
# Jupyter-wide policy regarding tags in the core projects

We really need to make a decision about core projects handle cell tags **before** the final release of notebook 5.1.

There has been ongoing discussion amongst @rgbkrk @takluyver @jasongrout @ellisonbg @Carreau and others regarding the purpose of tags… Right now we are not shipping any magic values with core Jupyter projects. 

**N.B.:** by "magic values", I mean values that are hard-coded and that map onto specific behaviour by default, usually with no ability for users to override this behaviour mapping.  

Notebook 5.1 includes a magic value (https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2549) which violates some of the expectations that had arisen in earlier discussions around allowed magic vlaues. 

## psuedo-namespaces with colons

@jasongrout and @ellisonbg had been extremely concerned about stepping on users' namespaces in any privileged jupyter tags. This would allow other projects/users to assign their own "namespace" by prefixing their tags with the namespace value postceded by a colon (e.g., `namespace:tag`). 

We had discussed the possibility of saving for jupyter the "null string" namespace (which would then require no `:`. This would make the `raises_exception` tag a candidate for meeting that namespace requirement. Unfortunately that is not the only value that matches the current "magic value".

## Potential problem: `"*raises_exception*"` matching

Right now, https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2549 makes the namespaces convention impossible. It includes *any* string that contains the characters `raises_exception` *anywhere* inside of it, regardless of the namespace+`:` that might prefix it. 

The value is not customisable, and is specified deep within the codecell execute functionality meaning it will not be able to be easily overwritten: 

https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/d4c6fe46cea2d7daa6dbe2647d766eec57ebb56e/notebook/static/notebook/js/codecell.js#L314-L325

### Compromise to resolve issues around https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2549 

I think the simplest suggestion would be to allow `raises_exception` and `nbval_raises_exception` as the two tags that the core `notebook` "knows" about. This greatly reduces namespace stompage while not breaking the behaviour that @takluyver and @mscuthbert introduced in merging https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2549. It's less than ideal to have a non-core project determining magic values inside the core project… but without making it configurable via a traitlet (or something), I can't think of another way to handle this. 

Even so it would seem to be a good idea to modularise this chunk of functionality so that it can be overwritten by external libraries without needing to rewrite the _entirety_ of `CodeCell.prototype.execute` just to access this one feature. 

## Document how tags are used outside of changelogs. 

Now that tags are supported in at least a couple of different ways in the core projects and in 3rd party projects, we should explicitly document those ways of using them. I list the ones I know about at the bottom of this issue. 

We need to start this effort and its closely tied to how we decide to handle the policy in general. Here are a couple of my initial thoughts:

1. [nbformat](https://github.com/jupyter/nbformat) should document the overall policy regarding tags. 
    1. We should have a policy for 3rd party libraries to know how to specify the tags they want to use; if we want to use namespaces with colons, we need to start promulgating that now.
    2. We should specify whether we intend that people should use tags as features that can apply to multiple cells or as unique identifiers
        a. `name`is specified in [`cell.metadata`](http://nbformat.readthedocs.io/en/latest/format_description.html#cell-metadata) as an explicit unique id across notebook
            i. we should have an interface similar cell tags that allows assigning a single unique name to each cell that will enforce the uniqueness condition as a part of the UI
2. [notebook](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook) docs should include screenshots of assigning tags to cells. 
    1. notebook docs should mention any magic values that tags have and their matching scope
3. [jupyterlab](https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyterlab) docs should include screenshots of assigning tags to cells. 
    1. jupyterlab docs should mention any magic values that tags have and their matching scope
4. [nbconvert](https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert) docs should describe how to specify tags for any of its purposes. 

### Examples of tags as user-defined values

* [nbconvert](https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert), via traitlet values , 
    * `TagRemovePreprocessor.remove_cell_tags`: removes cells with specified tags (https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert/pull/640)
    * `TagRemovePreprocessor.remove_input_tags`: removes inputs with specified tags (https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert/pull/643)
    * `TagRemovePreprocessor.remove_all_outputs_tags`: removes outputs with specified tags (https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert/pull/640)

### Examples of tags as hard-coded magic values

* [`notebook`](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook)
    * `"*raises-exception*"`: allows exceptions to pass https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2549
* [`computationalmodelling/nbval`](https://github.com/computationalmodelling/nbval/commit/6d1034139aaf3ea3f60f25d26748d058abb7816b): 
    * `"pytest-validate-ignore-output"` : avoids testing cell (deprecated)
    * `"nbval-ignore-output"`: avoids testing cell 
    * `"nbval-check-output"`: ensures testing cell even with `--nbval-lax`
    * `"nbval-raises-exception"`: allows exceptions to pass (analogous to notebook value)
    * `"nbval-skip"`: skips cell during testing 
* [nteract/papermill](https://github.com/nteract/papermill)
    * `"parameters"`: identifies a single unique cell (https://github.com/nteract/papermill/blob/master/papermill/execute.py#L157)

Are there other projects that the core in any way maintains that use tags in either of these ways? 

Regarding jupyterlab: @blink1073 @ian-r-rose @afshin are tags being used for anything meaningful in jupyterlab as of yet? Is there an official policy of how to handle them there?

issue_title: update the description for this repo
issue_body:
Can we discuss what the description should be for this repo?

Currently we have this for the description:

> The reference implementation of Blockstack

We also have this in the readme:

> Blockstack Core is the reference implementation of Blockstack.

With this language, we're implying that "Blockstack" refers only to the scope that Blockstack Core encompasses (the node backend software). If that were the case, it'd be appropriate to use separate brands for software that's not a part of Blockstack Core.

The way I see it is "Blockstack" refers to an entire suite of software, from the main server / node backend to the browser portal. Thus, we should avoid referring to Blockstack Core as a reference implementation of Blockstack.

In my view, Blockstack Core could be referred to as a reference implementation of Blockstack nodes or of the Blockstack server.

As a separate point, is there something that we want to communicate in the description for someone who suddenly happens upon https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core? What is most appropriate to include in the description?

Starting a conversation on this and tagging @muneeb-ali @jcnelson @guylepage3 @larrysalibra @pstan26 on this conversation.

issue_title: Agnostic support for CI systems
issue_body:
This is a follow up for https://github.com/buildbot/buildbot/pull/1671

I see the code from https://github.com/codecov/codecov-python/blob/master/codecov/__init__.py#L260-L430 is trying to hunt each CI system and extract the required information .

I don't see any generic lookup for those environment variables ... other than the token.

Those variables should also be VCS agnostic ... but for now this is of lower priority, since only GitHub/GitLab and Bitbucket are supported anyway.

The goal is to allow sending reports from Buildbot or other CI system without requiring to update codecov-python for each new CI system.

For my project I am using this environment in Buildbot https://github.com/chevah/buildbot-configuration-builder/blob/master/chevah/buildbot_configuration_builder/builder.py#L859

-----------

As agnostic environment variables I suggest:

commit -> VCS_COMMIT_ID
branch -> VCS_BRANCH_NAME
branch_url -> VCS_BRANCH_URL
build -> VCS_BUILD_ID
build_url -> VCS_BUILD_URL
pr -> GITHUB_PR_ID | GITLAB_PR_ID
slug -> GITHUB_SLUG | GITLAB_SLUG

----------

any CI system should set `CI=true` and maybe 'CI_NAME=service`

issue_title: Coverage of remote services
issue_body:
This is more of a question than a defect, but I'm just wondering if there is any way to use pytest-cov, maybe with the `--dist` flag, to gather metrics from code stored and executed on a remote server?

Here's the use case I have: I have a Python library I'm writing which interacts with a third party service. To test my library I have a pytest framework that launches a Docker container with the third party service installed on it, then I install my library inside the container, then I execute my test code. The testing logic then interacts with the third party service to flex the behavior of my library.

The problem here is that the Python runtime running my library isn't on my host / local machine, it's inside the docker container... and my scripts are invoked directly by the test API, but rather indirectly via the third party service running in the container.

So my question is: is there any way for me to set up the Python environment inside the Docker container to somehow run pytest and track the coverage metrics of any and all calls made to my library, and then accumulate those metrics and report them by the pytest runner running on the host?

issue_title: Enhance extras.image to accept a path/URL
issue_body: Feature enhancement: enhance extras.image to accept a path/URL as an alternative to a base64 encoded PNG. Implementation: check the content type of the extra content. If it is not base64 then just assume it to be a path/URL. The resulting href/src for the should be the provided string value as-is (optionally: could check if path starts with file: or http(s)) Background: In my setup, I do not have base64 encoded extra content when the report gets generated. Instead, my test logic already creates an external folder where screenshots are stored during test execution. So essentially I'd like to just link to them using a given file:// or relative URL inside the report, no matter if the report is self-contained or not. e.g. this ``` extra.append(pytest_html.extras.image('../screenshots/foo_bar.png')) extra.append(pytest_html.extras.image('file:///screenshots/foo_bar.png')) extra.append(pytest_html.extras.image('http://foo.bar/screenshots/foo_bar.png')) ``` would result in 3 tags in the report with their src/href set to the above values.

issue_title: shared/sys/backup endpoint
issue_body:
Hi All,
I took a first try at writing some code to handle using the backup worker capability that exists in BIG-IP. I use the worker in some of my scripts using requests, since it completes immediately and running the typical save sys ucs command will timeout, sometimes.

Could I get someone to check out my work? Not sure if I need to add anything else.
https://github.com/vsnine/f5-common-python/commit/4b1d74cf573c824b89d61bd3387b68ab39eed509

I've tested the following calls against v13.1.0.3, and it seems to work pretty well.
List all backups:
```
bkups = mgmt.tm.shared.sys.backups.get_collection()
for y in bkups:
    pp.pprint(y.raw)
```
Create a backup:
`makejob = mgmt.tm.shared.sys.backups.backup.create(name="backup1",file="backup1.ucs",action="BACKUP")`

Delete a backup:
```
bkupjob = mgmt.tm.shared.sys.backups.backup.load(name="fe6069cd-4d45-451b-8c81-2dfad02326ee")
bkupjob.delete()
```

Check in-progress job status:
```
checkjob = mgmt.tm.shared.sys.backups.backup.load(name="fe6069cd-4d45-451b-8c81-2dfad02326ee")
checkjob.refresh()
```

One thing I've seen is that if the same create call is made in rapid succession it seems to cause the jobs on the BIG-IP to stall, but I think it needs more testing.

Another thing is that I'm not sure how to make create() not require a `name` attribute. You can specify it, and it appears in the output when querying jobs, but the name attribute used in load() actually refers to the value of the `id` attribute. The URI for a given backup job looks like this: `/tm/shared/sys/backup/aeb1d552-cc9f-4a42-984e-ba03a6dcf0c1`


Thanks!

issue_title: Validate recipients as list or dict
issue_body: We currently don't do any input validation for recipients. It'd be pretty simple to check for list or dict and throw an appropriate exception. This will help avoid some confusion around the error received when a string, for example, is passed.

issue_title: _UNKNOWN_TOPIC error: How do I create a topic?
issue_body:
More of a question, really. If I do this in a python script:
```
conf = {'bootstrap.servers': '198.18.130.13',
        'group.id': 'mygroup',
        'debug': 'all',
        'socket.timeout.ms': 5000,
        'socket.max.fails': 1,
        'error_cb': my_kafka_error,
        'default.topic.config': {'message.timeout.ms': 4000},
        'session.timeout.ms': 3000}

kafka_producer = Producer(**conf)
kafka_producer.produce("Fred_topic", "Fred the dog went for a walk".encode('utf-8'), on_delivery=my_kafka_cb)
```
I get stuff like this in my output:
```
%7|1481562255.518|METADATA|rdkafka#producer-1| 198.18.130.13:9092/bootstrap: ===== Received metadata =====
%7|1481562255.518|METADATA|rdkafka#producer-1| 198.18.130.13:9092/bootstrap: 0 brokers, 1 topics
%7|1481562255.518|METADATA|rdkafka#producer-1| 198.18.130.13:9092/bootstrap:   Topic #0/1: Fred_topic with 0 partitions: Unknown broker error
%7|1481562255.518|METADATA|rdkafka#producer-1| 198.18.130.13:9092/bootstrap: Error in metadata reply for topic Fred_topic (PartCnt 0): Unknown broker error
%7|1481562255.518|STATE|rdkafka#producer-1| Topic Fred_topic changed state unknown -> notexists
%7|1481562255.518|PARTCNT|rdkafka#producer-1| Partitioning 1 unassigned messages in topic Fred_topic to 0 partitions
%7|1481562255.518|UAS|rdkafka#producer-1| 0/1 messages were partitioned in topic Fred_topic
%7|1481562255.518|UAS|rdkafka#producer-1| 1/1 messages failed partitioning in topic Fred_topic
************************** my_kafka_cb
************************** my_kafka_cb: error: code -188, name _UNKNOWN_TOPIC, str Fred the dog went for a walkI
************************** my_kafka_cb: msg: partition None, topic Fred_topic, value Fred the dog went for a walk
```

Note that in the above, "Fred_topic" was not registered or created in any way before the call to produce(). Am I supposed to be able to create a new topic from the confluent python client on the producer side? If so, how? Or is the idea that you must explicitly create topics out of band on the broker before you can produce() to them using the confluent python client?

issue_title: Fix Upstream Problems
issue_body:
This is meant as a placeholder for problems we notice upstream:
Note: these have only been observed when using the qtconsole:

- When the kernel language is python, the following is executed at startup:
    `try:\n    _usage\nexcept:\n    from IPython.core import usage as _usage\n  get_ipython().register_magic_function(_usage.page_guiref, 'line', 'guiref')\n    del _usage`
- At startup, `do_inspect` is called for the banner and the startup execute code.
- When executing code, `do_inspect` is called six times and `do_execute` is called twice
- `do_complete` is not always called when the TAB key is pressed (based on python syntax)
- The shell does not respect the `"codemirror_mode"` of the kernel

issue_title: Why rrulestr and rrule.__str__ omit RRULE paramter?
issue_body:
`str(rrule.rrulestr('DTSTART:19970105T083000\nRRULE:FREQ=YEARLY;INTERVAL=2;BYMONTH=1;BYDAY=SU;BYHOUR=8,9;BYMINUTE=30'))`
Gives:
`'DTSTART:19970105T083000\nFREQ=YEARLY;INTERVAL=2;BYMONTH=1;BYDAY=SU;BYHOUR=8,9;BYMINUTE=30'`

`str(rrule.rrule(freq=rrule.DAILY, dtstart=dt.datetime.now(), byweekday=rrule.MO(10), count=10))`
Gives:
`'DTSTART:20150526T004628\nFREQ=DAILY;COUNT=10;BYDAY=MO'`

Notice, that `RRULE` token is omitted everywhere.

Why? Is it in iCalendar RFC?

issue_title: Test Document Conversion media type detection
Content Editor doesn't escape html-like entities
issue_body:
Possible good but may be seen as weird behaviour: Type `©` or other escape strings into the content editor and update. It turns into a ©. Definitely bad: HTML markup isn't escaped Entering `

Header

`, updating, and it will stored as an h1 element. It will render in published page as an h1 element but not in the content editor, where it is uneditable. However update once more and it goes back to a paragraph. Anything that looks like an html tag gets swallowed. eg. if a writer wanted to add <> around something for stylistic reasons or because of the content of their article, it disappears!

issue_title: Powerline/zsh extremely slow in git repos with large file counts
issue_body:
Hey,

I'm finding zsh (powerline?) takes a long time to finish commands such as `ls`, `cd` in git repos with high file counts.. Is this a known issue? 

For example, the following project:

https://github.com/shakacode/react-webpack-rails-tutorial

Once npm install is run on this, the directory and its subdirectories will contain around 32000 files. The vast majority are `.gitignore`d, but the presence of so many files in a git repo seems to be the deciding factor for the massive slow downs I'm seeing - for example, Powerline/zsh are really speedy in the root directory, and in repos with a low file count. 

In the above project, the `client/node_modules` directory is the main offender, containing 29000 of the 32000 files. Note however that the problem is still exhibited in subdirectories outside of `node_modules`, eg `:/app/models`.

issue_title: Schema dump method does not validate?
issue_body:
According to the docs:

> Serialize objects by passing them to your schema’s dump method, which returns the formatted result (as well as a dictionary of validation errors...)

But as far as I can tell, the `Schema.dump` method does not perform validation.  I have:

```python
>>> from marshmallow import Schema
>>> from marshmallow.fields import String
>>> from marshmallow.validate import Regexp
>>> class TestSchema(Schema):
...     testfield1 = String(validate=Regexp('^foo'))
...     testfield2 = String(default='teststring')

>>> TestSchema().dump({'testfield1': 'bar'})
MarshalResult(data={'testfield2': 'teststring', 'testfield1': 'bar'}, errors={})
```

Based on the docs, I thought I would see validation errors in the `errors` key.  The `load` method validates as expected:

```python
>>> TestSchema().load({'testfield1': 'bar'})
UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={'testfield1': ['String does not match expected pattern.']})
```

But the `load` message does not apply `default` values:

```python
>>> TestSchema().load({})
UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={})
```

So the only way to get a validated data structure with default values applied appears to be:

```python
>>> result1 = TestSchema().load({'testfield1': 'foo'})
>>> result2 = TestSchema().dump(result1.data)
>>> result2.data
{'testfield2': 'teststring', 'testfield1': 'foo'}
```

The above is with:

```python
>>> marshmallow.__version__
'2.13.6'
>>> sys.version
'3.5.4 (default, Aug 23 2017, 18:32:05) \n[GCC 6.4.1 20170727 (Red Hat 6.4.1-1)]'
```

Am I simply misreading the documentation?

issue_title: miniterm.py fails to exit on failed serial port
issue_body:
Using minterm.py to connect to a serial USB device displays the following error when the device is unplugged:
```
Exception in thread Thread-1:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 810, in __bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 763, in run
    self.__target(*self.__args, **self.__kwargs)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pyserial-2.7-py2.7.egg/EGG-INFO/scripts/miniterm.py", line 245, in reader
  File "build/bdist.macosx-10.10-intel/egg/serial/serialposix.py", line 485, in read
    raise SerialException('read failed: %s' % (e,))
SerialException: read failed: [Errno 6] Device not configured
```
Unfortunately, miniterm does not exit at this point, and waits for a failure in sending before exiting. (i.e. You have to tap a key to quit.)

Minterm should probably exit immediately so that the connection can be reestablished without requiring user input.

(This is related to the PlatformIO issue https://github.com/platformio/platformio/issues/625#issuecomment-214032221 as PlatformIO uses miniterm to connect to systems under development.)

issue_title: Some Layer's hashcode method will throw null pointer exception, eg.TimeDistributed
issue_body:
Some Layer's hashcode method will throw null pointer exception, eg.TimeDistributed

Original code:

```scala
override def hashCode(): Int = {
    val state = Seq(super.hashCode(),
      layer, fInput, fGradOutput, inputSize, gradOutputSize, outputSize)
    state.map(_.hashCode()).foldLeft(0)((a, b) => 31 * a + b)
  }
```

We should change these layers to:
```scala
override def hashCode(): Int = {
    def getHashCode(a: Any): Int = if (a == null) 0 else a.hashCode()
    val state = Seq(super.hashCode(),
      layer, fInput, fGradOutput, inputSize, gradOutputSize, outputSize)
    state.map(getHashCode).foldLeft(0)((a, b) => 31 * a + b)
  }
```

issue_title: Request for new maintainer
issue_body:
I picked up the maintainer torch about a year ago when the project seemed to be stuck. I was using DJA on a side project, and I needed to fix a bug and get a new release out. Today, I'm no longer working on the side project and my interest in maintaining DJA has waned significantly.

As I'm not being a great steward for the project, I'd like to request that someone else pick it up. This is very much a community effort and code contributions come from a wide variety of contributors. The new maintainer would be most involved with:
1. Review of new Pull Requests and Issues.
2. Deploying new releases to PyPI.

I hope that I've added the right tools that can make management of this project easier for a future maintainer. Static code analysis has removed a lot of friction that might come up as a primary PR reviewer.

If you're in a decent place to step into the maintainer role, please let me know and I can work to transition over project handling to you. I'm happy to help with a bit of training if needed or improving release documentation.

issue_body:
Here is my code :

```python
    num_classes = 250
```

Here is the error :

issue_title: opendir raises a ResourceNotFound exception for a subdirectory which does exist.
issue_body:
```python
with open_fs('s3://my-bucket') as root_fs:
     with root_fs.opendir('a_real_directory') as dir_fs:
          print dir_fs.listdir(u'/')
```

Raises Not Found on the line which calls `opendir`

I find things to work entirely when using `file://` or `osfs://` protocol urls, but things tend to explode when using `s3://`. Because I've hit two show stopping issues in two attempts to use s3fs and pyfilesystem, I'm gonna conclude that the premise of this being a compliant `FS` implementation is false, untested, and broken.

It's a grand idea to write interchangeable filesystem code, best of luck!

issue_title: Add NWBRefSpec class
issue_body:
## 2) Feature Request

For consistency, it would be good to add a ``NWBRefSpec` ` class just so that all the spec classes from form have a counterpart in ``pynwb.spec``.  Currently this class would not need to define Iany custom behavior so it would be just a primitive class:

```
import pynwb.form.spec.spec.RefSpec

class NWBRefSpec(RefSpec):
    pass
```

### Problem/Use Case

The primary use-case is to simplify and improve consistency of imports needed when creating extensions to enable that all spec classes can be imported directly from ``pynwb.spec``.

- [X ] Have you ensured the feature or change was not already [reported](https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/issues/) ?

- [X] Have you included a clear description of the problem you are trying to solve?

issue_title: Pass row values to every Widget
issue_body:
AFAIU it will help create widgets with autocreation of related ForeignKey instances that require two fields already present in the row. For example:

* FK to `Person` with `first_name`, `last_name`
* FK to `Quote` that requires a `Book` (let's suppose it's identifiable just by name) and that book is already in the import fields
* FK to `POI` that is identifiable with two (or even three) coordinates

issue_title: Python3 AfricasTalkingGateway.py Changes
issue_body:
**AfricastalkingGateway.py Fixes**

- line 227 of AfricasTalkingGateway.py - changed print(response) instead of print response.

- import urllib2 is depracted in python3. The urllib2 and urllib in python2.x were combined and split across various modules in python3. Instead of:
`import` urllib2
import urllib
`

For python3:
`from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
from urllib.parse import urlencode
`

You can try wrapping it in a try-except block to check for both. e.g
`try:
        from urllib.request import urlopen
except:
        from urllib2 import urlopen
`
After doing this, it works. Danke!

issue_title: Proposal: search for local config-files/readers automatically
issue_body:
EUMETSAT has many Level 0, 1, and 2 products in different internal formats. These products are only used in-house and are not send out to the users. Therefore, `satpy`-readers for such products would quickly pollute `satpy/etc/readers/`-directory with `*.yaml`-files that are only useful for a small amount of PyTroll users.

The workaround with current implementation is to use `ppp_config_dir`-keyword when creating a `Scene`-object:
```
scn = Scene(fname, reader='my_reader', ppp_config_dir='/local/config/path')
```
This is a proposal for a possibility to add a local config directory where config files would be automatically searched. Proposed solution by modifying `config_search_paths`-method in `satpy/config.py` by setting an environmental variable, e.g. `SATPY_LOCAL_CONFIG`

#### Proposed modification

```python
def config_search_paths(filename, *search_dirs, **kwargs):
    # Get the environment variable value every time (could be set dynamically)
    # FIXME: Consider removing the 'magic' environment variable all together
    CONFIG_PATH = get_environ_config_dir()
    LOCAL_PATH = os.environ.get('SATPY_LOCAL_CONFIG')
    paths = [filename, os.path.basename(filename)]
    paths += [os.path.join(search_dir, filename) for search_dir in search_dirs]
    if LOCAL_PATH:
        paths += [os.path.join(LOCAL_PATH, filename)]
    # FUTURE: Remove CONFIG_PATH because it should be included as a search_dir
    paths += [os.path.join(CONFIG_PATH, filename),
              os.path.join(PACKAGE_CONFIG_PATH, filename)]

    if kwargs.get("check_exists", True):
        paths = [x for x in paths if os.path.isfile(x)]

    # flip the order of the list so builtins are loaded first
    return paths[::-1]
```

Any ideas, comments, thoughts? Would such modification be useful for other institutes/organizations? Or is this just silly? ;)

issue_title: Solar interpolation, straight wavelength bins
issue_body:
In the solar.py code you have this for interpolation in the method `interpolate`:

```python
xspl = np.linspace(start, end, (end - start) / self._dlambda)
```

Could it be that you wanted:
```python
xspl = np.linspace(start, end, round((end - start) / self._dlambda)+1)
```
because then, for a dlambda=0.001, for example, you would actually get 1 nm wide bins.
The round is required because linspace takes an integer as input there, to avoid floating point issues?

let me know, I can push another PR (but that, potentially could change some numbers on your side).

issue_title: If-Match:* behaviour is unclear (and crashes) with existing record

On 5.2 it would raise a "400" on existing record.

On current master, it will crash in "500" as it's not handled by `kinto.core.resource` anymore, but it's considered a valid header value by deserialization.

I think we should set the behavior to just ignore it. and process the request anyways. Opinions?

issue_title: Add custom (meta) data on buckets and collections
issue_body:
For some use-cases, it might become useful to be able to store some custom attributes in buckets or collections (e.g. metadata like application version, contact email or whatever).

Currently both Collection and Bucket resources do not define extra fields in their schema, and Cliquet drops unknown fields if not explicitly allowed.

We can either:
* Allow unknown fields in collection and buckets schemas
* Add a specific root level field (along ``data`` and ``permissions``)
* Add a specific field (called ``meta`` for example) in the schema that could receive anything.

The advantage of the latter is that custom fields do not interfere with anything in the protocol, and are trivial to implement. The inconvenient is having to put ``{data: {metadata: {email: "toto@yo.com"}}`` in the payload.

Thoughts ?

issue_title: `name` parameter is not used on first plotted line
issue_body:
When plotting a line with the ``vis.line()`` function, one can specify a ``name`` for a trace.
This works fine when `` 'appending' `` new lines, but does not work for the first line you plot in a window.

One can work around the bug by adding a ``legend`` entry to the options, but this is a dirty hack imo...
The reason this is irritating is because this does not allow for a consistent API for updating/removing traces.

# Reproducing bug
```python
import visdom
import numpy as np

vis = visdom.Visdom()
vis.line(np.array([1,2,3]), np.array([0,1,2]), win='my_window', name='custom_trace', opts={'showlegend':True})
# The line will have '1' as name in stead of 'custom_trace'
```

```
    read_ask_role = config_parser[profile_string].getboolean('google_config.ask_role', None)
AttributeError: RawConfigParser instance has no attribute '__getitem__'
```

because the python3 syntax for `RawConfigParser` to get a whole section via `config_parser['section name']` is not available in the native python2.7 `configparser`

A version-independent syntax would be `config_parser.getboolean(profile_string, item)` or `config_parser.getint(profile_string, item)`

csvgrep (others commands?) fail to quote output with vertical tab characters in input columns
issue_body:
I'm operating on the NPPES monthly CSV download (~4.8 million records) and am filtering and trimming the data using csvgrep and csvcut.  I pipe the results together.  It seems that when a cell contains a vertical tab (character code 11), the piping causes the data to be modified:

For instance, with [small file](https://github.com/onyxfish/csvkit/files/97850/fred.csv.gz), I run this command to see what data is in the raw file:

```
csvstat -c "Last Update Date" fred.csv.gz 
 38. Last Update Date
    <type 'datetime.date'>
    Nulls: False
    Values: 2010-02-26, 2008-06-25, 2013-03-27

Row count: 3
```

so in fred.csv, Last Update Date has three values.  

If I add a csvgrep and pipe it to csvstat:

```
csvgrep -c "Entity Type Code" -m "1" fred.csv.gz  | csvstat -c "Last Update Date"
 38. Last Update Date
    <type 'unicode'>
    Nulls: False
    Values: Y, 02/26/2010

Row count: 2
```

After the grep, I have a value (Y) in that column that does not appear in the original data.  It seems that CSV grep is not detecting that it needs to output a cell with a vertical tab with escaping.   There doesn't seem to be a way to force each cell to use quoting in the output.

Is there a way to force quoting in the output?

issue_title: latest version 0.9.0 does not allow compression of empty string
issue_body:
Previous versions of python-lz4 allowed compression of the empty string:

```
In [1]: lz4.compress('')
Out[1]: b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x88/\xc9\x0b'
```

the latest version fails:

```
In [2]: lz4.compress('')
lz4/__init__.py:18: DeprecationWarning: Call to deprecated function or method compress (use lz4.block.compress instead).
  def compress(*args, **kwargs):
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last) in <module>()
----> 1 lz4.compress('')

lz4/deprecated.py in new_func(*args, **kwargs)
     37             warnings.warn_explicit(msg, category=DeprecationWarning, filename=filename, lineno=lineno)
     38             warnings.simplefilter('default', DeprecationWarning)  # reset filter
---> 39             return cls_or_func(*args, **kwargs)
     40 
     41         return new_func

lz4/__init__.py in compress(*args, **kwargs)
     17 @deprecated('use lz4.block.compress instead')
     18 def compress(*args, **kwargs):
---> 19     return lz4.block.compress(*args, **kwargs)
     20 
     21 @deprecated('use lz4.block.decompress instead')

ValueError: Input source data size invalid: 0 bytes
```


Was there a reason for this change? We use lz4 for compression of data we write to a database. Some of the records might contain empty strings. If I were to upgrade to the latest version of lz4 I'd no longer be able to support datasets with empty strings, and I'd also no longer be able to decompress the compressed data that's already been written.

issue_title: Remove dependency on docker-py
issue_body:
SoS used `docker-py` for its access to docker. We have however gradually moved away from `docker-py` for a few reasons:

1.  The python api is less flexible than command line and more difficult to use.
2. The python api sometimes has inconsistent option names from the corresponding docker command, while the latter is usually what users would expect.
3. The python api has its own way of controlling output (`stream` stuff) which makes it difficult to implement our own options such as using `stderr` to redirect stderr.

Right now we have used `docker run` command instead of `docker-py` for the execution, but we still use `docker-py` for other actions such as `build`, `load`, and `pull`. This has several problems:

1. The `docker-build` action does not respect action options such as `stderr`.
2. Dependence on `docker-py` has made it difficult to deploy sos (#987 )
3. The hybrid approach is ugly from an implementational point of view anyway.

I would therefore like to completely remove dependency on `docker-py` and use native `docker` command for all its functions.

issue_title: Services from `/arlas/explore/*` accepting a filter in the `GET` or `POST` should also accept a filter in the `HEADER`
issue_body:
In gitlab by @sylvaingaudan on Aug 1, 2017, 11:57

Services from `/arlas/explore/*` accepting a filter in the `GET` or `POST` should also accept a filter in the `HEADER`.
The filter is specified in the JSON format and a `AND` operator is applied between the filter provided in the GET/POST and the filter provided in the `HEADER`.

Note: In some cases, the filter is currently appended as `OR`, if for example two `equal` operators are applied on a same field. This behavior is not acceptable for the present case : a real  `AND` operator must be applied.

issue_title: Nonetype not accepted as valid type by data saver
issue_body:
Let's consider the situation where we interleave a 1D and 2D sweep. This will look as follows.  

```python
import numpy as np

from qcodes.dataset.measurements import Measurement
from qcodes import Parameter

x = Parameter("x", set_cmd=None, get_cmd=None)
y = Parameter("y", set_cmd=None, get_cmd=None)

m = Parameter("m")
m.get = lambda:np.sin(x())

n = Parameter("n")
n.get = lambda:np.sin(x()**2 + y())

meas = Measurement()
meas.register_parameter(x)
meas.register_parameter(y)
meas.register_parameter(m, setpoints=[x])
meas.register_parameter(n, setpoints=[x, y])

with meas.run() as datasaver: 
    for ix in np.linspace(0, 6, 100): 
        x.set(ix)
        datasaver.add_result(("x", ix), ("y", None), ("m", m.get()), ("n", None))

        for iy in np.linspace(0, 6, 100): 
            y.set(iy)
            datasaver.add_result(("x", ix), ("y", iy), ("m", None), ("n", n.get()))
```

issue_title: Properly strip out trailing slash from BASE_URL
issue_body:
A user may include a trailing slash at the end of their `BASE_URL`. When we manually tack on `/api/v1` we are including a slash before `api`, which can end up forming strange URLs like this: `some.site//api/v1`.

While this works for most endpoints, our PaginatedList implementation chokes when trying to parse the `next` link header because Canvas returns back a URL with multiple slashes.

issue_title: Cannot invoke actor methods from other threads
issue_body:
### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution**: Ubuntu 16.04
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: source
- **Ray version**: 0.4.0
- **Python version**: 2.7.12
- **Exact command to reproduce**:

### Describe the problem
I'm wrapping some ROS subscriber logic into a Ray actor. I register a ROS callback on a ROS pub/sub topic. The callback is invoked in another thread when a message is received. In the callback, I want to invoke other Ray actors, but I can't do it because Ray can only invoke other actors from the main thread. Unfortunately, I can't force ROS to invoke the callback in the main thread.

I discussed with @robertnishihara , and this limitation is because the Ray API is currently not thread safe. Could you please make the API thread safe?

### Source code / logs
At @robertnishihara suggestion, I've temporarily disabled the main thread check from here: [https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/1475600c818bca4ba75b3085f4e45e8762096406/python/ray/worker.py#L1088-L1092](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/1475600c818bca4ba75b3085f4e45e8762096406/python/ray/worker.py#L1088-L1092)

However, it would be great if this issue could be sorted.

issue_title: GAF to RDF generation requires tests
issue_body:
Currently there are hardly any tests for RDF generation from GAF lines. The logic is complicated enough that we should work to produce a relatively sophisticated system that really defines the GAF-CAM data model and will catch any changes. We can accomplish this with a combination of Shex shape expression checks with PyShex and with SPARQL queries. 

The testing strategy is line by line, since gaf lines are largely independent from one another. 

1. Using a test GAF line, use rdfgen to produce RDF in an rdflib graph object
2. Use knowledge of the actual GAF annotation to decide what the the main triples should be (there will be either one or two)
3. Test that the triples exist with SPARQL. 
4. Use Shape Expressions and Shex to test the rest of the RDF graph, given that step 3 passes. This ensures that the triples referred to by the shapes are indeed the ones in step 3. 
Note: we could perhaps do some kind of meta shape, parameterizing on the specifics of the triples to utilize shapes more.

issue_title: Improvement TensorDB : remove hard dependency on mongoDB  and make it loose.
issue_body:
Given the general philosophy of TensorDB to connect DB based on query,
to remove strong dependency on DB framework.
TensorDB : Astract layer which encapsulates DB connection :  (Stream data, large Blob data, ...).

Having dependency on MongoDB is a pain, 
because we may use other DB framework: Big Query, Postgres, MySQL, Aurora, ....

It would be better to have general abstract layer and add specification for the DB platform
through engine definition (like in SQL Alchemy):
http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/core/engines.html

engine= create_engine(type="MongoDB", connection_string="   ")
engine= create_engine(type="filesystem", connection_string="   ")
engine= create_engine(type="AWSAurora", connection_string="   ")

db= TensorDB(engine=engine, .... )
...

It allows to add new connector without the need of changing the abstraction layer,
like in SQL Alchemy.

So, you can mix NO SQL DB with SQL DB as well as filesystem

issue_title: `set_clusters` should use string type for cluster labels for hdf5 dump
issue_body:
Hi,
when setting clusters from an external data frame (eg., read Seurat output using pandas with `vlm.set_clusters(my_annotation.loc[vlm.ca['CellID'],'cell.type'])`), the dtype of vlm.cluster_labels is `'O'`, which cannot be dumped into hdf5 (`TypeError: Object dtype dtype('O') has no native HDF5 equivalent`).
using `vlm.cluster_labels=vlm.cluster_labels.astype(np.string_)` fixes this problem but creates byte strings which is maybe not optimal. some unicode compatibility problem?

great package by the way!
